description:
Goal: co-derive the first useful root-scoped semantic facts from Compiler2-owned entry-dispatch and lowered-body facts. Semantic analysis must read compiler-owned body/dispatch data, not raw AST, and must not call the legacy whole-program lower/type/plan pipeline.

Change:
- Add direct semantic jobs over Compiler2-owned facts: `AnalyzeActivation`, direct callsite analysis, `ResolveDirectCall`, `DeriveReturnNeed`, `DeriveExecutable`, and the stricter `CheckSemanticClosure` needed for this first direct-call island.
- Activation identity includes root, function, and input shape. This ticket can use exact finite inputs for the first direct-call contracts; recursive widening policy lands in `fz-rh2.6.3`.
- Activation analysis starts from function entry dispatch plus lowered clause bodies, analyzes only dispatch-selected bodies, resolves through Compiler2 function/module identity, waits on missing entry-dispatch/helper/body facts, and publishes owned selected-callee and callee-activation facts.
- Return-need analysis handles the quicksort tuple destructure case: `partition/4` called from `{lo, hi} = ...` publishes a `TupleFields(2)` need and corresponding executable fact.
- `PlanExecutable` in this ticket is a small semantic fact projection, not backend planning: it records reachable lowered blocks/calls/return contracts needed before artifact work exists.
- Any remaining clause-head semantic logic in `LoweredBody` becomes a bug or cleanup target, not an alternate source of truth.

Why:
- This is where type facts and executable facts start settling in the same work graph. There is no separate type fixpoint followed by a planner fixpoint.
- Ordered dispatch belongs in shared dispatch facts; body semantics should consume the selected outcomes, not rebuild entry matching ad hoc.
- The semantic jobs own their outputs, so later redefinition can retract stale call edges by ordinary contribution replacement.

Checks:
- Quicksort plus uncalled `foo/0`, rooted at `main/0`, publishes selected direct call edges for the reachable quicksort path and keeps `foo/0` semantically cold.
- The `partition/4` call publishes a tuple return type/contract and `TupleFields(2)` return need before any artifact job exists.
- Root closure remains unresolved if a reachable dispatch/helper/callee definition/body is missing, resolved when all required semantic waits clear, and fatal only for real job errors with diagnostics already emitted through telemetry.
- Tests assert through telemetry/job effects; they do not inspect world internals.
- No `fz.frontend.lowered`, legacy `fz.type_infer.*`, `fz.planner.planned`, codegen, or artifact telemetry appears.

Non-goals:
- Recursive convergence and old-output retraction are `fz-rh2.6.3`.
- Runtime libraries, protocols, imports that require not-yet-indexed external code, callable reducers, and `Enum.reduce` are `fz-rh2.7.*`.
- Body-site dispatch planning (`case`/`with`/`receive`) is later work.
- Materialization, ABI, JIT/AOT/interp artifacts are `fz-rh2.8.*`.
- No fallback path into legacy whole-program lower/type/plan machinery.
title: Compiler2 semantic: derive direct-call facts from lowered bodies
